published_prior_art.cite: ColangeloLee2023ContinuousDML — localized cross-fitted continuous-treatment DML; MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased — fixed-window illness-death EIF

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_dose_response_minimax — fixed-dose pointwise Hölder two-point lower floor

published_prior_art.cite: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE — Hölder causal minimax lower-bound template; BonviniKennedy2022FastDoseResponse — local continuous-treatment dose-response rates
WestlingLuedtkeGilbertCarone2024Survival — cross-fitted survival inference uniform in event time; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment — uniform continuous-treatment bands
ChernozhukovChetverikovKato2014Gaussian gives sufficient changing-class couplings, not necessity for censored localized EIFs

structure_flags.label: Main minimax/inference kernel
structure_flags.one_line: All substantive open claims are labelled theorems; reclassify the upper/lower/inference headline as a conjecture so the proposal presents an open kernel rather than an unproved supporting theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Rate and honest-inference kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: The core omits closer continuous-treatment predecessors BonviniKennedy2022FastDoseResponse and KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017ContinuousDR, both directly relevant to pointwise smoothing and doubly robust continuous-treatment estimation.

novelty_flags.label: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased comparator promise
novelty_flags.one_line: The promise table calls the Martinussen result a strict tightening, but no statement formally gives a strict extension, frontier, or reduction against its published fixed-window result; either add that comparison or downgrade it to informed-by.

novelty_flags.label: thm:upper-rate through thm:ci-lower-length
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship rate and CI claims name predecessors but provide no section, theorem, equation, or quoted open-problem locator tying the cited work to the asserted gap.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand and lem:holder-version
soundness_flags.one_line: P_n is declared an observed-data law, yet theta_n=P_n(T_n^{u_star}<=t_0) invokes a potential outcome on an unspecified causal extension; causal-validity does not supply an observed-law functional or its identifying formula.

soundness_flags.label: def:one-step, def:interval, thm:gaussian
soundness_flags.one_line: The normalizer is ambiguous: sigma_hat is described as the empirical standard deviation of a localized score, while the Gaussian statement and interval require a standard error with the n^{-1/2} scaling made explicit.

soundness_flags.label: def:window-functional and phi_{P_n,b}
soundness_flags.one_line: The kernel mixture of exact-time risks is not connected to a defined stochastic hazard intervention, so it is not established that inserting an intervention density into the Martinussen--Knudsen--Rytgaard EIF targets theta_{n,b}.

soundness_flags.label: ass:localized-complexity
soundness_flags.one_line: “Satisfying the changing-class Gaussian-coupling condition at effective sample size nb” is not a stated mathematical predicate; specify the envelope, moments, entropy metric, covariance/anti-concentration, and required vanishing remainder.

soundness_flags.label: ass:weighted-product
soundness_flags.one_line: Its claimed applicability to delayed-entry survival learners is unsupported; the anchor itself identifies fully data-adaptive delayed-entry post-treatment survival learning as presently challenging, so this load-bearing condition needs a concrete verifiable learner regime or explicit conditional scope.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All rate and interval statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Known Hölder order and radius, an interior target time, one initiation, and CAR-compatible causal validity are accepted as restrictions of the model rather than claims of adaptation or general multistate validity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The minimax and honest-inference claims are conditional on this fixed interior Hölder/CAR single-initiation regime; unknown-smoothness adaptation and bounded-effective-count regimes are outside the headline.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper distinguishes observed-hazard and causally valid interventions, says exact-time treatment is not pathwise differentiable, and derives an EIF and cross-fitted one-step inference for a smoothed window intervention.
The paper derives a Hölder-model CATE minimax rate, a lower bound, and a rate-optimal local-polynomial causal estimator under stated conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business & Economic Statistics, published online 16 July 2025
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives kernel-localized doubly robust cross-fitted inference for continuous-treatment dose-response/partial-effect functionals with high-level nuisance conditions.
The paper studies continuous-exposure incremental interventions, derives EIFs and minimax lower bounds, and tracks effective sample size n/delta as the tilt concentrates.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(546), 2024
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper proposes a cross-fitted doubly robust treatment-specific survival estimator and establishes pointwise and uniform-in-time asymptotic linearity.
The paper supplies Gaussian approximation for suprema of empirical processes, including changing non-Donsker local classes under explicit moment and complexity conditions.
The paper constructs bias-aware honest local-polynomial confidence intervals, studies optimal bandwidths, and gives efficiency/length comparisons for nonparametric regression classes.
The paper proposes minimum-variance weighting with local-linear estimation, a uniform Bahadur representation, and bootstrap confidence bands for general continuous-treatment response functions.
The paper studies global and pointwise dose-response estimation, including a local doubly robust learner and mean-square-error rates under smoothness assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the causal extension and observed-law identification functional for g_P(u), then define the stochastic hazard intervention whose EIF is used and prove that it equals the kernel-mixture target.
Make sigma_hat an explicit cross-fitted estimate of sqrt(Var(phi_{P,b})/n), and state the variance-consistency and local score scaling conditions needed by the Gaussian and CI claims.

Add Bonvini--Kennedy and Kennedy--Ma--McHugh--Small with theorem/section anchors, and state a proposition showing exactly which censored multistate feature prevents their pointwise results from subsuming the proposed rate.

Replace the generic localized-complexity assumption with displayed CCK-style entropy, envelope, moment, anti-concentration, and nb_n remainder inequalities, then make the primitive-frontier OEQ target their necessity.

recommended_next_step: Revise the causal target/EIF bridge and score normalization first, then repair the survey/comparator chain before advancing to Stage 0.
